Root Cause Analysis

Co-occurrence clustering · Causal ranking · Jaccard similarity

v2/rca
Analyzing…
Advanced options

Events in the same bucket are co-occurrences

Min buckets a key must appear in

Lower → larger, looser clusters

How it works: Events are partitioned into time buckets. Keys that co-occur in the same bucket are linked by Jaccard similarity; pairs above the threshold are merged into clusters. When a failure key is given, co-occurring keys are ranked by how consistently they fire before the failure — positive lead time means a probable root cause, negative means a downstream consequence. Click any key tag in a cluster to instantly run RCA for that key.
Select a duration and click Analyze to discover co-occurrence clusters.
Add a failure key to rank probable root causes.
